mnemonics: PLUMS - Pluggable Look and Feel, Model-View-Controller (MVC), Thread Safety
keyPoints: Swing components use a modified MVC architecture, separating the data (model) from the visual representation (view) and user input handling (controller).
Swing is not thread-safe. All UI updates and interactions with Swing components must be performed on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) to avoid concurrency issues.
Swing's Pluggable Look and Feel (PLAF) allows applications to change their appearance at runtime without altering any code.
commonMistakes: Performing long-running operations on the EDT, which freezes the GUI and makes the application unresponsive.
Updating Swing components from background threads, which can cause unpredictable behavior and graphical glitches.
Confusing the MVC roles by putting business logic directly in UI components instead of in the model.
